The "Internet Awesomesauce" aesthetic refers to the playful and eclectic visual style found online, characterized by vibrant colors, exaggerated visuals, and surreal, often humorous elements. It embraces internet culture, meme references, and a sense of chaos, with an emphasis on individuality and creative expression. Weirdcore is an internet aesthetic that emerged in the early 2020s, characterized by its surreal, nostalgic, and often unsettling vibe. It draws inspiration from early internet imagery, low-quality graphics, and dreamlike or liminal spaces, creating a sense of familiarity mixed with unease. The aesthetic often features distorted or glitchy visuals, cryptic text, and mundane yet eerie scenes, such as empty hallways, abandoned playgrounds, or poorly edited photos with strange filters. Dreamcore is an internet aesthetic that recreates the feeling of being in a dream, especially the kind that is nostalgic, confusing, or slightly unsettling. It often features familiar places like empty hallways or playgrounds, but presented in a distorted or eerie way, along with blurry visuals and random, cryptic text. Instead of making logical sense, dreamcore focuses on emotion and atmosphere, making things feel both real and unreal at the same time—like a memory you can’t fully understand bu

Webcore (or Old Web) is an aesthetic expresses nostalgia for Internet culture of the early 1990s to early 2010s. Webcore utilizes retro web/tech design elements (i.e. screencaps, GIFs, clip-art, video games) combined with aspects of poetry and self-expression.

Weirdcore is a surreal internet-based aesthetic and art movement from the early 2020s, featuring low-quality, amateurish photography or digital art that evokes feelings of disorientation, nostalgia, and mild dread. It mimics the amateur, unpolished look of the late 1990s/early 2000s internet to disrupt visual comfort.
